skill.name: skills-factory
skill.description: Create, validate, package, and improve Claude Code skills with scaffold scripts, quality checks, deployment guidance, and evaluation workflows.
skill.summary: Build production-ready Claude Code skills with templates, validation scripts, packaging tools, and evaluation guidance.
skill.author: DaronVee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, and secret-key patterns, but most are Markdown examples, validation regexes, or expected local tooling for a skill factory. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious network behavior was found. Publication is reasonable with a warning because bundled scripts can create, package, install, and overwrite local Claude skill directories.

medium_findings.title: Local Skill Installation Can Overwrite Existing Skills
medium_findings.description: The packager installs a selected skill into personal or project Claude skill directories. If the target skill exists, it prompts for confirmation and then removes the existing target before copying the new directory. This is expected behavior for a deployment tool, but users should only run it on trusted skill folders.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem write and removal behavior is directly implemented and semantically tied to installation. User confirmation and a scoped target path reduce the likelihood of accidental broad deletion.

medium_findings.title: Packaging Includes All Files Under the Skill Directory
medium_findings.description: The packaging script walks every file under the supplied skill directory and writes it into a zip archive. This is necessary for distribution, but it can include unintended local files if the source directory contains secrets, generated artifacts, or unsafe links.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The archive behavior is explicit, but exploitability depends on what the user places inside the source directory. No evidence of automatic exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Mostly Operational Examples
low_findings.description: The numerous command execution findings are primarily fenced shell examples that show users how to initialize, validate, package, and deploy skills. They are relevant to the skill purpose and do not show hidden command execution by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown usage examples, not executable code paths. This strongly supports a false-positive classification for most static command hits.

low_findings.title: API Documentation Uses Environment Variables for Keys
low_findings.description: Deployment documentation shows curl calls that read an API key from an environment variable. This is a normal authentication pattern, but users should avoid pasting real keys into shared logs or committed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key is referenced through an environment variable in documentation examples. No code sends collected secrets to an unexpected endpoint.

low_findings.title: Recursive Delete Example Is Scoped to a Skill Directory
low_findings.description: Deployment guidance includes a recursive delete command for replacing one personal skill directory. The path is scoped to a named skill, but users should verify placeholders before running deletion commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is real and destructive if copied incorrectly, but the documented target is not root or the entire home directory. This is a safe-context warning rather than malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Confirmed Filesystem Removal Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The installer removes an existing target skill directory after an explicit overwrite confirmation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shutil.rmtree call is present in the overwrite path. The target is derived from the Claude skills directory and selected skill name.

dangerous_patterns.title: Confirmed Archive-All-Files Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The packager recursively adds every regular file under the skill directory to the distribution zip.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The recursive packaging loop is clear. Risk depends on user-controlled directory contents rather than hidden behavior.

content.value_statement: Creating reliable AI skills requires structure, validation, and repeatable testing. This skill provides scaffolding, packaging, deployment guidance, and evaluation workflows for Claude and Codex users.
actual_capabilities: Scaffolds new skill folders with SKILL.md, scripts, references, and assets directories.
Validates YAML frontmatter, naming rules, descriptions, references, scripts, and workflow structure.
Packages skill directories into zip files for Claude.ai, Claude Desktop, or API upload.
Installs skills into personal or project Claude Code skill locations after validation.
Guides iterative testing with a two-Claude builder and reviewer methodology.
Provides troubleshooting guidance for triggering, validation, packaging, and deployment issues.
limitations: It focuses on Claude Code skill structure and may need adaptation for other agent platforms.
Local scripts can write, copy, package, and overwrite files in Claude skill directories.
It does not replace manual security review for third-party skills or bundled scripts.
Skills still need separate deployment for Claude Code, Claude.ai, Desktop, and API surfaces.

use_cases.title: Create a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Generate a consistent starting structure and validation workflow for a project-specific Claude Code skill.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Skill for Distribution
use_cases.description: Validate a completed skill, package it as a zip file, and follow deployment steps for each Claude surface.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Skill
use_cases.description: Use evaluation notes, workflow patterns, and troubleshooting guidance to make a skill trigger and perform more reliably.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Simple Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a small skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skills-factory to help me plan a simple Claude Code skill for [task]. Ask the key design questions first, then propose the folder structure.

prompt_templates.title: Scaffold and Validate
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a new skill and check its structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skills-factory to scaffold a skill named [skill-name], then review the generated SKILL.md for description quality, trigger terms, and validation readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Deployment
prompt_templates.scenario: Package and deploy a completed skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skills-factory to validate and prepare [skill-path] for distribution across Claude Code, Claude.ai, Claude Desktop, and the Claude API.

prompt_templates.title: Run an Iterative Evaluation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced improvement cycle for a production skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use skills-factory to design a two-Claude evaluation cycle for [skill-name]. Define test tasks, observation criteria, failure signals, and targeted edits.

output_examples.input: I need a skill for reviewing database migrations before release.
output_examples.output: A proposed skill structure with a clear trigger description, workflow sections, validation checkpoints, and reference files for migration review standards.

output_examples.input: My skill does not trigger reliably in Claude Code.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting review that checks description trigger terms, examples, workflow clarity, and progressive disclosure references.

output_examples.input: Package this skill for my team and the API.
output_examples.output: A validation-first deployment checklist covering project installation, zip packaging, API upload requirements, and separate surface verification.

best_practices: Keep SKILL.md focused and move detailed guidance into one-level reference files.
Run validation before packaging or installing a skill in personal or project locations.
Test skills with realistic tasks and record specific failures before editing instructions.
anti_patterns: Do not package directories that contain unrelated secrets, credentials, or generated artifacts.
Do not rely on one deployment surface to sync skills to every Claude product.
Do not leave TODO placeholders or vague trigger descriptions in production skills.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It helps create Claude Code skill folders, supporting references, optional scripts, validation workflows, and distributable packages.

faq.question: Can it install skills automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. The packaging script can install validated skills into personal or project Claude Code skill directories.

faq.question: Does it work for Claude.ai and Claude Desktop?
faq.answer: It can package a skill as a zip file for upload, but each surface still needs separate deployment.

faq.question: Is this a security review tool?
faq.answer: No. It validates structure and quality patterns, but third-party skills still need manual security review.

faq.question: Why does it include scripts?
faq.answer: The scripts automate scaffolding, validation, packaging, and local installation tasks for skill development.

faq.question: What should I check before packaging?
faq.answer: Confirm that the skill folder contains only intended files, no secrets, clear instructions, and valid references.
